# TBR Data Capabilities — Autotask PSA + Datto RMM
### Prepared for the Tribros Transportation Technology Business Review
*Source: Triple Cities Tech platform integrations · Generated by code/architecture review, not assumptions*

---

## 0. TL;DR (read this first)

- **Autotask is the system of record for tickets.** It can give us *every* ticket Tribros has ever opened, with full breakdowns by **queue, priority, status, issue type, source, technician, hours, and response/resolution times**. Three years is no problem — Autotask holds the full history.
- **Datto RMM gives us the "we're protecting your environment 24/7" story:** how many devices we manage, what they are (servers vs. workstations, OS), patch/AV/health status (current snapshot), and the volume of monitoring **alerts** we caught and resolved.
- **One important caveat (no sugar-coating):** *our website's database only keeps a rolling ~30-day window of tickets.* It is **not** a 3-year archive. To get 3 years we pull **directly from the Autotask API** (which we can do — see §6). The number you'll quote in the TBR comes straight from Autotask, not from our cached copy.
- **Datto RMM device/patch/AV numbers are a "today" snapshot**, not a 3-year history. Datto **alerts** are historical but only as far back as Datto retains them (typically months, not 3 years — to be confirmed against the live account).

Everything below is the full menu. Pick what you want on the slides; I've suggested a layout in §5.

---

## 1. What lives where (so we quote accurate numbers)

| Data | Source of truth | History depth available | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tickets + all ticket attributes | **Autotask PSA API** | Full (3+ years) | Pulled live, paginated — every ticket |
| Time entries / labor hours | **Autotask PSA API** | Full | Billable vs. non-billable flagged |
| Ticket notes (for response-time math) | Autotask PSA API | Full | Used to compute first-response time |
| Company service tier / classification | Autotask PSA API | Current | e.g. "Platinum Managed Service" |
| Contracts | Autotask PSA API | Current + history | Contract type/name (renewal context) |
| Managed device inventory | **Datto RMM API** | Current snapshot | Counts, OS, type, online status |
| Patch / antivirus / reboot status | Datto RMM API | Current snapshot | Per-device hygiene |
| Monitoring alerts | Datto RMM API | Retention-limited history | Open + resolved, typed, timestamped |

> **Why the 30-day caveat exists:** our scheduled sync (`syncTickets`) only keeps a short rolling window in the local DB to stay inside Vercel's function timeout. The three built-in report engines (Business Review, Annual Service Report, Executive Summary) read from that local DB, so out of the box they'd only "see" ~30 days for a company that hasn't been backfilled. The fix is a **direct 3-year Autotask pull** (§6), which bypasses that window entirely.

---

## 2. AUTOTASK — every TBR-relevant metric we can pull

### 2A · Company & contract context (the "who/what" header for the deck)
- **Service tier / classification** (e.g., Platinum/Gold Managed Service)
- Active status, primary phone/web, # of active contacts on file
- **Contract(s):** name, type, dates — useful framing for a renewal conversation

### 2B · Ticket volume — the headline numbers
- **Total tickets created (last 3 years)** ← *the headline count you asked for*
- **Tickets per calendar year** (e.g., 2023 / 2024 / 2025 / 2026-YTD) — shows the trend
- **Tickets per month** (12/24/36-month trend line)
- Tickets **closed/resolved** vs. **created** (net change)
- **Currently open** backlog, and **aging** (open > 7 days, open > 30 days)

### 2C · Ticket breakdowns — "what kinds of tickets / queues" (your core ask)
- **By Queue** — *this is your "what kinds of queues" answer.* Resolved to real names (e.g., **Service Desk, Network Monitoring, Security / SOC, Projects, Onboarding**, etc.), with count + % of total. Queue names are already translated in our system.
- **By Priority** — Critical / High / Medium / Low, count + %
- **By Status** — current disposition (New, In Progress, Waiting Customer, Complete, …)
- **By Issue Type & Sub-Issue Type** — Autotask's problem categorization (e.g., Hardware, Software, Network, Email, Account/Access). *Stored as codes today; their labels are fully resolvable from Autotask and the export tool translates them.*
- **By Source** — how the ticket arrived: Phone, Email, Client Portal, **Monitoring Alert (proactive)**, etc. This lets us split **reactive vs. proactive/maintenance** work — a strong value story.

### 2D · Service performance — the "value we deliver" metrics
- **Average & median first-response time** (how fast we acknowledge)
- **Average & median resolution time** (overall **and per priority**)
- **First-touch resolution rate** (% solved in a single interaction)
- **SLA compliance %** (where SLAs / due dates are set on tickets)

### 2E · Effort / labor invested
- **Total support hours logged** for Tribros over the period
- **Billable vs. non-billable** hours (a lot of MSP value is non-billable — worth showing)
- Hours **per month** and **per technician**
- **# of distinct technicians/engineers** who worked their account (depth of bench)

### 2F · Ticket-level appendix (optional, if they want receipts)
- A line-item export: ticket #, title, queue, priority, status, created, closed, hours — useful as a backup tab, not a slide.

---

## 3. DATTO RMM — every TBR-relevant metric we can pull

### 3A · Managed footprint (current snapshot)
- **# of managed devices** for Tribros' site(s)
- **Servers vs. workstations** (and laptop/desktop split)
- **By operating system** (Windows Server, Windows, macOS, Linux)
- **# online / reachable** right now

### 3B · Patch & security hygiene (current snapshot)
- **Patch status distribution** — fully patched vs. pending vs. missing approvals
- **Total patches installed** across the fleet
- **Antivirus** product + status coverage
- **Reboot-required** count
- (Available if useful) per-device **software inventory**

### 3C · Proactive monitoring — the "always watching" story
- **Total monitoring alerts processed** in the period, and **% resolved**
- **Open alerts** right now
- **Alerts by type** (offline, disk space, patch, AV, performance, …)
- **Alerts by priority/severity**
- **Monthly alert trend**
- **Top alerting devices** (which machines need attention — upsell hook)
- **Patch-related alert volume** (proof of patch management effort)

> Device/patch/AV figures reflect the environment **as of the pull date**. Alert counts are historical to the limit of Datto's retention.

---

## 4. Bonus layers we also integrate (include only if Tribros is on these services)

Our platform also has live connectors for the broader security stack. Pull these only if Tribros actually subscribes — otherwise they'll show as "not covered" (which itself can be a **gap-to-upsell** slide):

- **Datto EDR** — endpoint threat detections / responses
- **DNSFilter** — malicious / policy-violating web queries blocked
- **Datto BCDR** — backup appliance health, # of protected servers
- **Datto SaaS Protection** — M365 / Google Workspace seats backed up
- **SOC Analyst Agent** — security incidents identified & triaged for them
- **Microsoft 365 user-protection** — login/impossible-travel monitoring, MFA coverage, Secure Score, external-sharing & mailbox-rule auditing, dark-web credential monitoring

---

## 5. Suggested TBR slide map (metrics → slides, to drive renewal)

1. **Cover / relationship summary** — service tier, years as a client, # devices managed, # users supported.
2. **"By the numbers" (3-year)** — total tickets, total support hours, total alerts handled. Big bold numbers.
3. **Ticket volume trend** — per-year (and per-month) line; story = "stable/declining volume = improving stability," or "growth = expanding footprint."
4. **What we worked on** — tickets **by queue** + **by issue type** (pie/bar). *Directly answers "what kinds of queues / security / network monitoring."*
5. **Reactive vs. proactive** — tickets by source (monitoring-generated vs. user-reported).
6. **How fast & how well** — avg first response, avg resolution, first-touch rate, SLA %.
7. **Endpoints we protect** — device counts, servers/workstations, OS mix, patch compliance %, AV coverage.
8. **24/7 monitoring** — alerts processed & resolved, monthly trend, top alerting devices.
9. **Security posture** (if applicable) — EDR/DNS/BCDR/SaaS/SOC/M365 coverage.
10. **Recommendations / gaps** — anything unmanaged, unpatched, or not yet on a security layer = renewal/upsell.

---

## 6. How we get the ACTUAL Tribros numbers

The three existing report engines read the local DB (30-day window), and **this working session has no live API credentials**, so the numbers can't be pulled from here directly. The reliable options:

- **Option A — One-shot export tool (recommended).** I add a small, read-only endpoint that pulls Tribros' full 3-year ticket history **straight from the Autotask API** (bypassing the 30-day window), resolves every queue/priority/status/issue-type/source label, computes all the metrics in §2, and layers in the Datto RMM device + alert data from §3. It returns both raw JSON and a clean printable report. You run **one** PowerShell command with the `MIGRATION_SECRET` you already have; nothing sensitive is shared in chat. (Optionally, you hand me the preview URL + secret and I run it and format the finished numbers for you.)
- **Option B — Paste credentials, I pull it now.** You paste the Autotask + Datto API keys into the chat and I pull the 3 years immediately and hand back finished numbers. Fastest, but exposes long-lived secrets in chat.
- **Option C — Document only (this file).** You take this menu and pull the numbers later using your own tools.

*The exact Autotask company name doesn't need to be confirmed up front — the tool matches on a partial name (e.g., "Tribros" or "Transport") and reports which company it matched so you can verify.*
